name: The last supper
notes: The institution of the Lord's supper, told three times in the Gospels and once by Paul — the earliest written account of the four (1 Corinthians). Matthew and Mark run together almost word for word. Luke and Paul form the other pair: both alone carry "this do in remembrance of me," both phrase the cup as "the new testament in my blood" taken after supper. Matthew alone adds "for the remission of sins"; Paul alone adds "ye do shew the Lord's death till he come." John, at this point in the meal, tells the washing of feet instead and is not part of this weave.
